decks.notes: The deck focuses on using Amulet of Vigor to untap lands and ramp quickly into Primeval Titan, allowing for explosive plays with lands like Crumbling Vestige and Lotus Field. The goal is to assemble the Amulet Titan combo quickly and efficiently, utilizing cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Scapeshift to search for key pieces. The deck has a toolbox approach with lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Urza's Saga, enabling different strategies depending on the opponent. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful land-based combos and win the game in a short amount of time.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the necessary lands and combo pieces to ramp up mana and bring out Primeval Titan to search for powerful lands such as Urza's Saga and Vesuva. The key strategy is to abuse the Amulet of Vigor to untap lands and generate additional mana, allowing for explosive plays and setting up for a game-winning Scapeshift or Cultivator Colossus combo. The deck includes ways to protect key pieces and ensure consistency in setting up the combo, creating a versatile and powerful win condition.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy through various means such as counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. By controlling the board with counters like Counterspell and Spell Snare, removing threats with Fatal Push and Field of Ruin, and drawing cards with Consult the Star Charts and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and eventually win the game through attrition. Additionally, cards like Cling to Dust and Sheoldred's Edict provide additional disruption and graveyard hate to further disrupt the opponent's game plan.

This deck focuses on fast, aggressive gameplay with cheap and efficient spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The goal is to play small creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler early on, and then pump them up with spells like Mutagenic Growth and Violent Urge to maximize damage output. Card selection and manipulation are important with cards like Preordain and Expressive Iteration to ensure a smooth flow of threats. The deck also includes burn spells like Lightning Bolt to finish off the opponent or clear the way for creatures.

This aggro-affinity deck focuses on quickly deploying artifacts onto the battlefield to overwhelm opponents with explosive bursts of damage. It utilizes powerful artifacts such as Mox Opal, Engineered Explosives, and Urza's Saga to create a formidable board presence. Emry, Lurker of the Loch aids in recurring artifacts while Metallic Rebuke provides disruption for opposing spells. The deck also includes versatile removal options like Tormod's Crypt and Claws of Gix to handle a variety of threats. With a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells, this deck aims to win quickly and decisively.

The deck revolves around quickly filling the graveyard with high-value creatures, then bringing them back to the battlefield using reanimation spells like Persist and Ephemerate. Quantum Riddler and Psychic Frog help dig through the deck for combo pieces, while Thoughtseize and Prismatic Ending provide disruption against opponents. Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones are powerful threats that can close out the game quickly once they hit the battlefield. The deck aims to out value opponents by leveraging its graveyard and powerful creatures to control the game and secure a win.

This deck focuses on utilizing Birthing Ritual to ramp up quickly and overwhelm the opponent with early aggression. The deck includes a mix of creatures with disruptive abilities and mana-efficient spells to control the board and maintain pressure. By using the ramp from Birthing Ritual and the versatility of cards like Shardless Agent and Force of Negation, the deck aims to outpace opponents and maintain control of the game. The inclusion of cards like Subtlety and Endurance allows for additional disruption and protection, making it difficult for opponents to deal with the deck's threats.

The deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Tower) to generate a large amount of colorless mana quickly. It uses cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Thought-Knot Seer to control the opponent's hand and board state, while also having Eldrazi creatures like Sire of Seven Deaths and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger to provide a powerful win condition. The deck also includes disruption elements like Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere to disrupt the opponent's game plan and maintain control of the game.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Allosaurus Rider combo using Eldritch Evolution and Neoform to tutor for powerful creatures like Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant and Griselbrand, while also utilizing cards like Ureni, the Song Unending and Planar Genesis for added value. The deck also includes toolbox cards like Endurance and Consign to Memory to disrupt opponents' strategies. Additionally, the deck has ramp in the form of Disciple of Freyalise and Generous Ent to help accelerate the combo. Overall, the deck aims to efficiently combo off and overwhelm opponents with big creatures.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling a board state with cheap artifacts in order to generate value and pressure the opponent, utilizing cards like Mox Opal, Mishra's Bauble, and Engineered Explosives. The Affinity strategy is supported by cards like Kappa Cannoneer and Weapons Manufacturing, allowing for explosive plays and overwhelming the opponent. The deck also includes disruption elements like Metallic Rebuke and Pithing Needle to control the game and protect the aggro plan. Urza's Saga provides additional threats and card advantage, while cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Arcbound Ravager provide utility and synergy with the artifact theme.

The deck focuses on quickly amassing artifacts on the battlefield to power up abilities and create explosive board states. By utilizing cards like Engineered Explosives, Mox Opal, and Mishra's Bauble, the deck can generate great amounts of mana and card advantage. It also uses cards like Arcbound Ravager and Pinnacle Emissary to enhance the power of its artifacts. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy, while also being able to adapt to different matchups through the flexibility of its artifact-based synergies.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to put pressure on the opponent quickly and efficiently through aggressive creature play, while also utilizing burn spells and combat tricks to control the board and deal direct damage. The deck focuses on utilizing creatures with haste and abilities that generate value upon attacking, along with tools like Goblin Bombardment to close out games. Additionally, the inclusion of planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provides additional reach and versatility, making it a well-rounded aggressive deck.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly defeat the opponent. It utilizes cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to apply pressure on the opponent while also using Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to deal direct damage. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Voice of Victory to close out the game. The strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient aggro plan while maintaining board presence and control.

This deck aims to use cycling creatures like Street Wraith and Curator of Mysteries to fill up the graveyard quickly, then cast Living End to bring them all back onto the battlefield for a powerful board presence. The deck also utilizes cascade cards like Shardless Agent to outvalue opponents and disrupt their game plan. The inclusion of force of negation and Endurance helps to protect the combo and provide some interaction against opposing strategies. Overall, the strategy revolves around cycling cards and cascading into Living End to overwhelm the opponent with creatures.

This deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with key creatures like Griselbrand and then using cards like Goryo's Vengeance to bring them back onto the battlefield for a powerful combo finish. It also includes cards like Ephemerate and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The inclusion of Thoughtseize and Force of Negation helps protect the combo pieces while Otherworldly Gaze and Psychic Frog provide card selection and filtering. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful reanimation strategy.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo without relying on lands. It utilizes cards like Preordain, Hydroelectric Specimen, and Lotus Bloom to set up the combo, while cards like Orim's Chant and Sea Gate Restoration provide protection and card advantage. The key combo piece is Goblin Charbelcher, which can deal huge amounts of damage to the opponent. Tameshi, Reality Architect and Thundertrap Trainer are also crucial in helping to assemble the combo. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent with cards like Disrupting Shoal and Stern Scolding while setting up its win condition.
